message:
chore(publish): replace path deps with crates.io versions, add sub-crate metadata

Prepare workspace for crates.io publishing by removing path overrides
and enriching sub-crate metadata:

Cargo.toml (workspace root):
- asupersync-macros: drop path = "asupersync-macros" — resolve from
  crates.io registry instead of local path for reproducible builds
- asupersync-conformance: drop path = "conformance" in both [dependencies]
  and [dev-dependencies] sections
- This enables consumers to depend on published versions without needing
  the full workspace checkout

asupersync-macros/Cargo.toml:
- Add readme = "README.md" field for crates.io listing page

conformance/Cargo.toml:
- Add readme = "README.md" field
- Add keywords: async, conformance, runtime, testing
- Add categories: asynchronous, development-tools::testing

New files:
- asupersync-macros/README.md: brief description of proc macro crate purpose
- conformance/README.md: brief description of conformance test tooling

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -5,6 +5,7 @@ edition = "2021"
 license = "MIT"
 description = "Proc macros for asupersync structured concurrency runtime"
 repository = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/asupersync"
+readme = "README.md"
 keywords = ["async", "structured", "concurrency", "macros", "proc-macro"]
 categories = ["asynchronous", "concurrency"]

@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# asupersync-macros
+
+`asupersync-macros` provides proc macros used by the
+[`asupersync`](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/asupersync) runtime.
+
+## Purpose
+
+The crate supports structured-concurrency ergonomics in the main runtime by
+expanding macro syntax into compile-time checked Rust code.
+
+## License
+
+MIT

@@ -5,6 +5,9 @@ edition = "2021"
 description = "Conformance test suite for async runtime specifications"
 license = "MIT"
 repository = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/asupersync"
+readme = "README.md"
+keywords = ["async", "conformance", "runtime", "testing"]
+categories = ["asynchronous", "development-tools::testing"]
 
 [dependencies]
 serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }

@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# asupersync-conformance
+
+`asupersync-conformance` provides fixtures and checks for validating async
+runtime behavior against the asupersync conformance expectations.
+
+## Purpose
+
+This crate is intended for conformance testing and report generation used by
+the asupersync project and compatible runtime implementations.
+
+## License
+
+MIT
